name: Rochester Medical Transportation Inc
fleet_size_est: unknown (grew from single van; small-to-mid fleet)
districts_or_customers: Medicaid members; dialysis/day-care/medical appointment riders in Rochester and Buffalo metros
recent_news: No notable 2024-2026 M&A or news found; company expanded service area from Rochester to also cover Buffalo.
fit_notes: Pure NEMT/ambulette operator with no clear school-bus or SPED school-contract footprint. Limited strategic fit as a Summit school-bus tuck-in; could be relevant only if Summit pursues an adjacent NEMT/SPED-medical platform play in upstate NY.

name: Med-Van Transport
fleet_size_est: small (vans + ALS/BLS ambulances; specific count not disclosed)
districts_or_customers: Hospitals, nursing homes, rehab centers, dialysis clinics in Cambria/Indiana counties PA
recent_news: No M&A news 2024-2026; active member of Bedford County Chamber and PEHSC; currently hiring EMTs/paramedics.
fit_notes: Small ambulance/NEMT operator, not a school-bus contractor. Same Northern Cambria address as Tri County Transportation (rank 75) suggests possible related-party situation worth checking. Weak direct fit for Summit's school-bus tuck-in strategy.

name: Modivcare Inc
owner_or_parent: Secured creditors post-Chapter 11 (formerly public NASDAQ: MODV)
principals: Anne Bailey (listed President in source data; corporate leadership reshuffled post-restructuring)
fleet_size_est: broker model — does not own meaningful fleet; subcontracts to thousands of NEMT providers
employees_est: thousands enterprise-wide (record shows 100 at this office)
districts_or_customers: Medicaid managed care plans nationally; CO counties Adams/Arapahoe/Boulder/Broomfield/Denver/Douglas/Jefferson/Larimer
recent_news: Filed Chapter 11 Aug 20, 2025 with ~$1.4B debt and $2.79B 2024 revenue; plan effective Dec 29, 2025; ~$1.1B debt eliminated; delisted from NASDAQ; equity wiped out and company emerged owned by former secured creditors.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate. Modivcare is a multi-billion-dollar national NEMT broker (not a school-bus operator) and just emerged from Chapter 11 under creditor ownership. Should be removed from Summit's tuck-in pipeline; could be relevant only as a potential channel partner for SPED/medical adjacent flow.

name: Johannes Bus Service Inc
fleet_size_est: mid-size — small-to-full-size school buses, special-needs buses, charter coaches (specific count not disclosed)
districts_or_customers: Three area school districts in the Illinois Quad Cities (Rock Island/Moline area); originally founded under a verbal contract with a local Catholic school
recent_news: No 2024-2026 M&A news found; remains family-owned with 60+ year operating history; active Quad Cities Chamber member.
fit_notes: Attractive tuck-in profile: 60+ year family operator, three-district contract base, integrated school/SPED/charter mix, 125 employees. Founder-generation succession likely a live question; Quad Cities (IA/IL border) gives Summit a Midwest expansion foothold. Source-data revenue ($37M) looks inflated vs. employee count — diligence needed.
